Trendshift — Project-by-Project Analysis

1. obra/superpowers (157.6k ⭐)

  • Category: AI dev tools
  • Business Model: Open-source framework — likely monetizes via consulting/enterprise
  • USP: First credible "agentic skills framework" — defines how AI agents learn reusable skills
  • Target: Developers building AI coding workflows

2. anthropics/claude-code (115.8k ⭐)

  • Category: AI coding assistant
  • Business Model: Freemium SaaS (API usage), drives Anthropic API revenue
  • USP: Terminal-native, understands full codebase, handles git workflows
  • Target: Professional developers

3. rustdesk/rustdesk (112k ⭐)

  • Category: Self-hosted tools
  • Business Model: Open-source core + paid hosting/enterprise features
  • USP: Self-hosted TeamViewer alternative — privacy-first
  • Target: IT teams, privacy-conscious orgs, SMBs

4. NousResearch/hermes-agent (97.9k ⭐)

  • Category: AI agent framework
  • Business Model: Open-source model + enterprise services
  • USP: "Grows with you" — adaptive agent that learns user patterns
  • Target: AI builders, developers

5. garrytan/gstack (74.9k ⭐)

  • Category: AI workflow / dev tools
  • Business Model: Open-source configuration — thought leadership play by YC chief
  • USP: Garry Tan's personal Claude Code setup — CEO/Designer/Eng Manager agent roles
  • Target: Startup founders, indie hackers

6. VoltAgent/awesome-design-md (58.5k ⭐)

  • Category: Design / AI coding
  • Business Model: Open-source collection — drives traffic to VoltAgent
  • USP: Drop-in DESIGN.md files that give AI coding agents brand-specific taste
  • Target: Frontend developers using AI coding tools

7. forrestchang/andrej-karpathy-skills (54.7k ⭐)

  • Category: AI coding optimization
  • Business Model: Open-source — community contribution
  • USP: Distills Karpathy's observed LLM coding pitfalls into a CLAUDE.md file
  • Target: Claude Code power users

8. paperless-ngx/paperless-ngx (38.4k ⭐)

  • Category: Document management / Self-hosted
  • Business Model: Open-source, community-supported (donations/sponsorships)
  • USP: Scan-to-archive pipeline with OCR and full-text search
  • Target: SMBs, home users, privacy-conscious organizations

9. openai/openai-agents-python (22.9k ⭐)

  • Category: AI agent framework
  • Business Model: Open-source SDK — drives OpenAI API usage
  • USP: Official OpenAI multi-agent framework — lightweight and Pythonic
  • Target: Developers building multi-agent systems

10. addyosmani/agent-skills (17.3k ⭐)

  • Category: AI dev tools
  • Business Model: Open-source by Google eng lead — thought leadership
  • USP: Production-grade (not toy) engineering skills for AI coding agents
  • Target: Engineering teams adopting AI coding

TrustMRR — Project-by-Project Analysis

1. Stan ($3.58M MRR, 1% growth)

  • Category: Creator economy / Platform
  • Business Model: Platform fees (marketplace model) — takes cut of transactions
  • USP: Full-stack creator monetization — not just courses, a livelihood platform
  • Target: Creators, solopreneurs, course sellers

2. TrimRx ($323K MRR, 18% growth)

  • Category: Health / Telehealth
  • Business Model: Telehealth subscription — medication + consultation fees
  • USP: GLP-1 focused (Semaglutide/Tirzepatide) — riding the Ozempic wave
  • Target: US adults seeking weight loss

3. Rezi ($282K MRR, 2% growth)

  • Category: Career tools / SaaS
  • Business Model: Freemium SaaS + Rezi Enterprise (B2B)
  • USP: "Best resume builder" reputation + enterprise for 300+ orgs including Fortune 500
  • Target: Job seekers, universities, enterprise HR

4. Kibu ($234K MRR, 53% growth)

  • Category: Health tech / Vertical SaaS
  • Business Model: SaaS subscription per provider org
  • USP: Purpose-built for I/DD care — EHR + compliance + teaching tools in one
  • Target: Provider orgs serving intellectual/developmental disabilities (48 states)

5. Cometly ($214K MRR, 5% growth)

  • Category: Marketing analytics
  • Business Model: SaaS subscription
  • USP: AI chat with your ads data — marketing attribution specifically for SaaS companies
  • Target: SaaS marketing teams

6. Supliful ($198K MRR, 4% growth)

  • Category: E-commerce / Creator economy
  • Business Model: Transaction fees — consumers pay at purchase, no upfront costs for creators
  • USP: Full CPG brand in a box — product selection through fulfillment
  • Target: Creators wanting physical product lines

7. DM Champ ($190K MRR, 4% growth)

  • Category: AI Sales / MarTech
  • Business Model: SaaS subscription + white-label reseller model
  • USP: AI that closes high-ticket sales autonomously — $1K deposits on $40K treatments
  • Target: Agencies (white-label), high-ticket service businesses

8. Dealsourcr ($149K MRR, 30% growth)

  • Category: Real estate / SaaS
  • Business Model: SaaS subscription
  • USP: Deal sourcing with 30% growth — likely real estate or M&A lead gen
  • Target: Real estate investors, deal finders

9. PROSP ($128K MRR)

  • Category: Sales automation
  • Business Model: SaaS subscription
  • USP: AI-powered LinkedIn outreach automation
  • Target: B2B sales teams, agencies

10. Postiz ($96.6K MRR, 18% growth)

  • Category: Social media management
  • Business Model: Open-source core + hosted SaaS
  • USP: "Agentic" social media scheduler — AI-driven content scheduling
  • Target: Social media managers, indie hackers, small businesses

11. SeoBOT ($74.8K MRR, 6% growth)

  • Category: SEO / AI tools
  • Business Model: SaaS subscription
  • USP: Full AI agent for SEO — keywords, research, blog, pSEO
  • Target: Content-driven businesses, SEO agencies

12. Codédex ($84.4K MRR, 2% growth)

  • Category: EdTech
  • Business Model: Freemium — learn to code platform
  • USP: Gen Z-focused coding education (likely gamified, mobile-first)
  • Target: Gen Z learners, beginners

Conclusions

Top Trends (Cross-Platform)

  1. AI Agent Skills & Frameworks dominate GitHub — Over 60% of trending repos are AI agent configurations, skills, or frameworks. The meta-layer (teaching AI how to work) matters more than the AI itself.

  2. Claude Code Ecosystem is exploding — Multiple top-25 repos are Claude Code skills, configs, and tools. This is the fastest-growing developer ecosystem right now.

  3. AI Sales & Marketing Automation prints money — DM Champ ($190K), PROSP ($128K), SeoBOT ($75K), Cometly ($214K) — AI selling and marketing is the highest-revenue category.

  4. Health tech MRR is quietly massive — Stan ($3.6M), TrimRx ($323K), Kibu ($234K), Medical Practice ($149K) — healthcare/telehealth dominates the revenue charts.

  5. Self-hosted & privacy-first sees sustained demand — RustDesk (112k ⭐), paperless-ngx (38.4k ⭐), thunderbolt — open-source alternatives to commercial tools keep growing.

Money vs Hype Gap

Hyped (GitHub stars, low monetization) Quietly Printing Money
AI agent skills/frameworks (huge stars, mostly free) Stan ($3.6M MRR — creator platform)
Leaked system prompts (14.6k ⭐, $0) TrimRx ($323K — GLP-1 telehealth)
3D scene reconstruction models Kibu ($234K, 53% growth — I/DD care)
Open-source RADAR (13.8k ⭐) DM Champ ($190K — AI sales closer)
Phased array RADAR (cool but niche) Dealsourcr ($149K, 30% growth)

Key insight: The gap between GitHub hype and actual revenue is enormous. Projects with massive star counts generate zero direct revenue, while unsexy vertical SaaS in healthcare and sales automation quietly generates millions.

Opportunities

  1. AI Agent Configuration as a Service — Everyone's making CLAUDE.md files and skills. Nobody's selling a curated, tested, maintained collection as a subscription. The "dotfiles for AI" market.

  2. Vertical AI Sales Agents — DM Champ proves AI can close $40K treatments. Apply the same pattern to other verticals: real estate, legal, consulting, B2B SaaS. White-label AI closer.

  3. GLP-1 Adjacent Products — TrimRx at $323K MRR with 18% growth. The weight-loss drug economy needs companion tools: nutrition tracking, side-effect management, progress analytics.

  4. I/DD & Specialized Care Tech — Kibu at 53% growth in an underserved niche. Applied to other specialized care verticals (autism support, elderly care, chronic disease management).

  5. SEO for AI/LLM Visibility — SeoBOT and AEO Engine both focus on LLM visibility. This is a new category — making brands discoverable by ChatGPT, Perplexity, etc. First-mover advantage.

Quick Takes

  • Claude Code is the new VS Code. The ecosystem around it (skills, configs, frameworks) is where all developer energy is flowing. If you're building for devs, build for Claude Code.

  • "AI selling to humans" is the highest-ROI application. Not code generation, not art — autonomous sales agents closing high-ticket deals. DM Champ's pitch says it all: "AI sells like your best closer, 24/7."

  • Healthcare MRR is underserved and lucrative. The top revenue list is dominated by health plays, yet GitHub trending has zero health projects. Massive opportunity gap between developer attention and where the money flows.

Report generated 2026-04-19 by Trend Scout agent. Sources: trendshift.io, trustmrr.com.
